onboarding.step1Title: What kind of reader are you, right now?
onboarding.step1Desc: No judgment, no pressure. This just helps us calibrate suggestions and reading plans to where you actually are.
onboarding.step2Desc: Pick up to 5 genres you love. This tunes what the Oracle reaches for first — you can change these anytime in your profile.
onboarding.step3Title: What are you looking for at the moment?
onboarding.step3Desc: Pick up to 3. This is the mood we'll lean into for suggestions this week — it's easy to update later.
onboarding.uploadReplace: Tap to replace, or continue below
onboarding.uploadClickToChoose: or click to choose a file
onboarding.uploadHowToSteps: → click "Export Library" → wait a moment → download the CSV.
onboarding.uploadNoFile: Don't have one yet? You can {link} and add books manually later.

1.title: Casual companion
1.sub: A book a month or two. I like a story that pulls me along — cozy fantasy, thrillers, page-turners.

2.title: Steady reader
2.sub: A book or two a month. Open to most genres if the writing's good. Not afraid of a slow start.

3.title: Devoted reader
3.sub: Reading is a major part of my life. I'll go anywhere a great book takes me — literary, weird, dark, demanding.

4.title: Literary appetite
4.sub: I'll wrestle with Faulkner and Han Kang. Difficult prose is part of the pleasure.

5.title: Voracious + experimental
5.sub: I want to be undone. Bring me the prose that breaks itself open — Donoso, Lispector, Cărtărescu.

level-up.title: Level up my reading
level-up.sub: Stretch me. Build a path that takes me from where I am to something harder, deeper, weirder.

explore.title: Get into a new topic or genre
explore.sub: Introduce me to a category I haven't explored — Korean literature, folk horror, Latin American gothic — with a guided path.

random.title: Just give me something to read
random.sub: I'm not trying to grow. I want a good book each month, suited to my taste. Surprise me.

onboarding.step4Title: Bring your reading history with you.
onboarding.step4Desc: Export your Goodreads library and drop the CSV here. We'll fill your virtual library with what you've already read so suggestions can be smarter — and so the shelves don't start empty.
onboarding.step5Title: What are you hoping to get from this?
onboarding.step5Desc: This shapes the kind of reading plans we'll suggest. You can change it anytime in your profile.

comfort.title: A comfort read
comfort.sub: Familiar, gentle, low-stakes. Something to unwind into.

challenge.title: A real challenge
challenge.sub: Dense prose, big ideas. I want to work for it.

escapism.title: Total escapism
escapism.sub: Take me somewhere else entirely — plot, world, momentum.

mind-bending.title: Something mind-bending
mind-bending.sub: Structure, twist, or premise that rewires how I think.

character-driven.title: Character-driven
character-driven.sub: I want to live inside someone else’s interior life.

atmospheric.title: Atmosphere and mood
atmospheric.sub: Heavy on tone and setting — gothic, eerie, immersive.

fast-paced.title: Fast-paced
fast-paced.sub: Propulsive. I want to tear through it.

short-read.title: Something short
short-read.sub: A slim volume or novella — I need a quick win.

about.subtitle: Why this exists, what it does, and where it's going.
about.originBody1: Most readers I know have an Amazon wishlist, a Goodreads pile, a Notes app full of titles, a stack on the nightstand. Hundreds of books in queue — and somehow, every Friday night, we still spend twenty minutes deciding what to read next.
about.originBody2: The Oracle started as a personal experiment to fix that. The premise: a wishlist shouldn't just be a hoarding pile. It should be the input to a recommendation engine that already knows your taste, because you built it yourself.
about.originBody3: That experiment grew into something larger — a full reading companion with clubs, shared sessions, progress tracking, and an AI that doesn't just suggest books but helps your group decide what to read together.
about.evolutionItem1Title: v1–v2 — HTML batches and vanilla JS
about.evolutionItem1Body: Instagram screenshots of book recommendations became styled HTML pages. Then a single-page app in plain JavaScript with localStorage — the first Oracle category and similarity matching. Worked, but didn't scale past one device.
about.evolutionItem2Title: v3–v25 — React, sync, and the reading stack
about.evolutionItem2Body: React with Vite, Supabase for cross-device sync, Google authentication. The Oracle grew to two modes (category and similarity), AI-powered reading plans arrived, and the full reading lifecycle took shape: Wishlist → Read Next → Currently Reading → Library.
about.evolutionItem3Body: Book Clubs launched the app's first multi-user feature: shared reading groups, sessions tied to a specific book, real-time member progress tracking. Discussion threads, admin-pinned questions, Oracle-powered conversation starters, and group polls for deciding what to read next followed in the next release.
about.featuresIntro: Each feature is here to answer a different version of "what should I read next?" — alone or with others.
about.featureWishlistBody: Your full pile of books you intend to read someday. Pre-seeded from a curated catalog of over a thousand titles, but fully editable. Books leave the wishlist automatically when you mark them as read. Bulk Goodreads CSV import available from your Profile.
about.featureLibraryBody: Everything you've finished, with star ratings, optional notes, and read dates. The Oracle draws on your library to understand your taste. Filter by genre, sort by rating or date, browse by series.
about.featureReadNextBody: A short queue — the books you've actually committed to reading next. Different from the wishlist, which is aspirational. Drag to reorder, move to Currently Reading in one tap.
about.featureCurrentlyReadingBody: Track books actively in progress with a start date and page-level progress. A live progress bar shows how far through each book you are. Finish a book from here and it moves to your Library with your rating.
about.featureOracleBody: The recommendation engine, with two ways to draw books from your vault:
about.featureOracleCategoriesBody: Pick a temperament — folk horror, gothic, Latin American literary fiction, cozy fantasy — and the Oracle draws books for you to choose from.
about.featureOracleSimilarBody: Tell the Oracle one to three books you've loved. It finds others with the same blood in them.
about.featureOracleModes: Both modes work in two ways: Vault-only draws only from your wishlist (fast, deterministic). AI-powered calls Claude and may surface books beyond your vault, with reasoning.
about.featurePlansBody: A reading plan is a small, structured commitment: "finish this series," "read three books on grief," "work through this author's catalog." Plans are AI-generated from a prompt, ordered by the Oracle, and shareable via URL. Multiple active plans are supported.
about.featureListsBody: Create curated reading lists from any books in your collection — a genre deep-dive, a gift recommendation, a themed shelf. Lists can be made public and shared via a link that renders beautifully for anyone, no account needed.
about.featureClubsBody: Create a reading group, invite members via a private join link, and read together. Admins create Sessions — each tied to one book with a date range and notes for the group. The session page shows every member's live reading progress against the book.
about.featureDiscussionBody: Sessions have full discussion threads: free comments, admin-pinned questions with their own answer threads, and one level of replies. Tap "☩ Oracle suggests" for AI-generated discussion questions tailored to the book. Club polls let the group vote on what to read next — or let the Oracle suggest three candidates and turn them into a poll automatically.
about.featureDashboardBody: Your home screen. Currently Reading books front and center, active Book Club sessions at a glance, all reading plans below, and a paginated activity feed showing your recent reading history.
about.roadmapBody: The Oracle isn't trying to become Goodreads. The destination is something more opinionated: a reading companion that knows your taste deeply, helps your group read together, and uses AI where it actually adds value — not everywhere.
about.feedbackBody: If there's anything broken or any new features you would like to see, send us a message at support@thebooksoracle.com
about.roadmapTierNear1Body: When a session ends, the Oracle generates a short summary of the group discussion, overall sentiment on the book, and a suggested next read. Shareable as a page.
about.roadmapTierNear2Body: Personal annotations tied to specific pages or chapters, private by default, optionally shared with your club.
about.roadmapTierMed1Body: Reading Plans already build genre journeys on demand. Next: editorial entry points — "Start here for Gothic horror" — curated picks that are there before you ask.
about.roadmapTierMed2Body: The Oracle already speaks Spanish everywhere you meet it. Next: enriching the catalog itself with Spanish-language prompts, significantly improving data quality for Latin American titles.
about.roadmapTierFuture1Body: A companion for nonfiction and demanding reads: capture what stayed with you after each session, link ideas across books, and let the Oracle resurface them when they matter.
about.pricingNote: Payments processed securely by Lemon Squeezy. We never store card details.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.whoWeArePara1: The Books Oracle (accessible at thebooksoracle.com) is a personal reading companion application operated by an individual developer based in Costa Rica.
privacy.whatDataWeCollectPara1: Account data. When you sign in with Google, we receive your name, email address, and profile photo. We store these to identify your account.
privacy.whatDataWeCollectPara2: Reading data. Your library, wishlist, read-next queue, ratings, notes, reading dates, and progress belong to you and are stored in your account.
privacy.whatDataWeCollectPara3: Usage data. We track how many times you use AI features per month to enforce free-tier quotas and monitor infrastructure costs. This counter resets monthly.
privacy.whatDataWeCollectPara4: Subscription data. If you subscribe to Pro, we store your Paddle customer ID and subscription ID. We never store card numbers or payment details — all payment processing is handled by Paddle.
privacy.whatDataWeCollectPara5: Club data. Your display name, reading activity, and any posts or discussion contributions in book clubs are visible to other club members.
privacy.whatDataWeDoNotCollectPara1: We do not collect your location, device identifiers, browsing history outside the app, or biometric data. We do not run advertising and have no advertising partners. We do not sell your data to any third party.
privacy.howDoWeUseYourDataPara1: Your reading data powers app features — displaying your library, generating Oracle recommendations, building reading plans, and tracking progress. Your email may be used for transactional notifications if you opt in. We do not send marketing emails without explicit consent.
privacy.howDoWeUseYourDataPara2: AI features send prompts to Anthropic 's Claude API. These include book titles and genre preferences but not your name, email, or any personally identifying information.
privacy.dataStoragePara1: Your data is stored in Supabase (PostgreSQL on AWS) with row-level security policies ensuring users can only access their own data. All data is transmitted over HTTPS. Service role credentials are stored only in server-side environment variables and are never exposed to the client.
privacy.thirdPartyPara1: We use: Google OAuth (sign-in only), Supabase (database), Anthropic Claude (AI features), Paddle (payments — Merchant of Record), Netlify (hosting), Hardcover / OpenLibrary (book metadata).
privacy.yourRightsPara1: You can delete your account and all data at any time from Profile → Reset profile. You can request a copy of your data or ask us to delete specific records by emailing support@thebooksoracle.com. We respond within 30 days. EEA users have GDPR rights including access, rectification, erasure, and portability.
privacy.cookiesPara1: The app uses browser localStorage and sessionStorage to cache reading data locally for performance. These are not advertising cookies and do not track you across other sites. No third-party tracking cookies are set.
privacy.childrenPara1: The Books Oracle is not directed at children under 13. If you believe a child has provided personal data, contact us and we will delete it promptly.
privacy.changesPara1: We may update this policy from time to time. Significant changes will be noted in the in-app release notes.

terms.title: Terms & Conditions
terms.acceptancePara1: By creating an account or using The Books Oracle (thebooksoracle.com), you agree to these Terms. If you do not agree, please do not use the service. These terms form a binding agreement between you and the operator of The Books Oracle, an individual developer based in Costa Rica.
terms.theServicePara1: The Books Oracle is a personal reading companion providing library tracking, AI-powered book recommendations, reading plans, and book clubs. We provide the service on a best-effort basis and reserve the right to modify or discontinue any part of it with reasonable notice via in-app release notes.
terms.yourAccountPara1: You must sign in with a valid Google account and be at least 13 years old. One account per person — creating multiple accounts to circumvent quota limits or other restrictions may result in account termination.
terms.acceptableUsePara1: You agree not to: circumvent AI quotas via multiple accounts; reverse engineer or scrape the service at scale; use the service unlawfully; post harmful content in clubs; or attempt to access other users' data.
terms.yourContentPara1: Your reading data belongs to you. You grant us a limited license to store and process it solely to provide the service. Content you share in book clubs is visible to other club members and is your responsibility. You can delete your account and all content at any time from Profile.
terms.aiFeaturesAndAccuracyPara1: Oracle recommendations, reading plans, and categorizations are AI-generated and provided for entertainment and discovery. They may contain inaccuracies. We do not guarantee accuracy, completeness, or suitability of AI-generated content.
terms.subscriptionsAndPaymentPara1: The Pro plan is billed monthly at $5 USD via Paddle, who acts as Merchant of Record. Subscriptions renew automatically until cancelled. You can cancel from Profile at any time; access continues through the current billing period. We reserve the right to change pricing with 30 days notice to active subscribers.
terms.intellectualPropertyPara1: The The Books Oracle application, design, and original content are owned by the operator. Book metadata is sourced from third-party catalogs and subject to their respective licenses.
terms.disclaimerAndLimitationOfLiabilityPara1: The Books Oracle is provided "as is" without warranties. We are not responsible for data loss, service interruptions, or inaccuracies in AI-generated content. Our total liability for any claim shall not exceed the amount you paid us in the preceding 12 months.
terms.governingLawPara1: These terms are governed by the laws of Costa Rica. Questions: support@thebooksoracle.com

refunds.title: Refund Policy
refunds.ourApproachPara1: We want you to be happy with The Books Oracle Pro. If you subscribed and feel it isn't right for you, we will make it right.
refunds.day14MoneyBackGuaranteePara1: If you subscribe to The Books Oracle Pro and are not satisfied for any reason, you can request a full refund within 14 days of your initial purchase — no questions asked.
refunds.day14MoneyBackGuaranteePara2: Email support@thebooksoracle.com with subject "Refund request" and the email address on your account. We will process the refund within 5 business days.
refunds.renewalsPara1: Monthly renewals are not automatically refundable after they are charged. Cancel before the renewal date from your Profile page to avoid the next charge. If a renewal charge occurred due to a technical error on our part, contact us within 7 days for a full refund.
refunds.serviceOutagesPara1: If The Books Oracle experiences a significant outage affecting AI features for more than 48 consecutive hours, subscribers may request a prorated credit or refund for the affected period.
refunds.howRefundsAreProcessedPara1: Payments are processed by LemonSqueezy. Refunds return to the original payment method and typically take 5–10 business days to appear. After a refund, your subscription downgrades to the free tier. Your reading data is retained.
refunds.howRefundsAreProcessedPara2: After a refund, your subscription downgrades to the free tier. Your reading data is retained.
refunds.exceptionsPara1: We reserve the right to decline refund requests in cases of clear abuse, such as repeatedly subscribing and refunding or using multiple accounts to circumvent limits.
refunds.contactPara1: For refunds or billing questions: support@thebooksoracle.com — we typically respond within 1–2 business days.

notFound.title: The Oracle can't see where you're going.
notFound.desc: This path leads nowhere the Oracle can find. The page may have moved, or never existed at all.

sitemapPage.title: Sitemap — The Books Oracle
sitemapPage.desc: A map of every section of The Books Oracle.
sitemapPage.heading: Sitemap
sitemapPage.intro: Everything The Books Oracle has to offer. Wishlist, library, and profile pages are personal to each reader, so you won't find those listed here — sign in to see your own.

intro.ariaLabel: The Books Oracle — an oracle's reading
intro.line1: Anyone can show you the bestsellers.
intro.q1: But do they know what you truly long for?
intro.q2: Can they trace the thread through every book you've loved?
intro.q3: Do they help you read deeper — not just more?
intro.q4: Could they find the one no one thought to show you?

hero.eyebrow: A reading companion for the dark academia at heart
hero.headline: An Oracle that knows what you'll love next.
hero.subheadline: Track what you've read, get recommendations that actually fit your taste, and read with people instead of alone.
hero.imageAlt: The Books Oracle dashboard — reading goal, currently-reading list, and Oracle recommendations

problem.heading: Reading shouldn't feel like a second job
problem.point1: Your next book is scattered across a wishlist, a Goodreads shelf, and a note on your phone you'll never reopen.
problem.point2: Algorithmic feeds are optimized for engagement, not for you — recommendations that chase trends instead of your taste.
problem.point3: Reading became solitary by default, not by choice — there's no easy way to read *with* people.

features.heading: A tour, not a promise
features.subheading: Everything below is already built and live in the app.

oracle.title: The Oracle
oracle.subtitle: Recommendations that get you
oracle.description: Tell the Oracle your genres and mood and it reads your library like a librarian who knows you — match-scored picks, not a bestseller list.
oracle.slide1Body: Pick a genre and a source — your own wishlist, the Vault, or the Oracle itself — and get a new book in seconds.
oracle.slide2Body: Tell us the books you've loved, and the Oracle finds what belongs next to them on your shelf.
oracle.slide3Body: Describe a mood — a rainy, gothic afternoon — and the Oracle returns four picks drawn from your taste.

readingLife.title: Your Reading Life
readingLife.subtitle: Everything you're reading, in one place
readingLife.description: Wishlist, currently-reading with progress, and your full library — no more scattered notes and forgotten tabs.
readingLife.slide1Body: Every book you've saved, cover to cover, in one browsable place.
readingLife.slide2Body: Add books in a tap and browse your wishlist by category whenever the mood strikes.
readingLife.slide3Body: Tag your books your way, and always find them grouped exactly how you think about them.
readingLife.slide4Body: Log pages as you go and watch the progress bar fill — pick up exactly where you left off, every time.

plans.title: Reading Plans
plans.subtitle: Plan your reading, month by month
plans.description: Let the Oracle build a reading plan that levels up your reading, finishes a series, or gets you started on a new genre.
plans.slide1Body: Tell the Oracle what you're after and how much time you have — it drafts a plan made specifically for you.
plans.slide2Body: Track your progress at a glance and move books straight from your plan into your read-next queue.

friends.title: Friends & Activity
friends.subtitle: See what your people are reading
friends.description: Follow friends, watch their shelves fill, and turn “what should I read next” into a conversation instead of a guess.
friends.slide1Body: See what friends finish, start, and rate as it happens.
friends.slide2Body: Open a friend's profile to browse their recent reads.
friends.slide3Body: Build a list with — or for — your friends, and share it publicly or keep it just for you.

stats.title: Stats & Goals
stats.subtitle: Watch your reading year take shape
stats.description: A yearly goal, your pace, and the genres you actually gravitate toward — gentle numbers that reward the habit instead of nagging you.
stats.slide1Body: Watch your progress build alongside the genres you keep coming back to.
stats.slide2Body: A month-by-month chart reveals the shape of your reading year.
stats.slide3Body: Small daily wins add up — and the streak keeps you honest.

clubs.title: Book Clubs
clubs.subtitle: Read together, session by session
clubs.description: Create a club or discover one, set a reading pace, and discuss each session without spoilers racing ahead of the group.
clubs.slide1Body: Let the Oracle draft a smart poll for your next pick, or discussion prompts for your next session.
clubs.slide2Body: See the current book, which chapters are in play, and who's along for the ride.
clubs.slide3Body: Find groups by taste and pace, then join in a single tap.
clubs.slide4Body: Discussion threads unlock session by session, so nobody races ahead of the group.

howItWorks.heading: Three steps to your next favorite book
howItWorks.step1Body: Genres, moods, the books you already love.
howItWorks.step2Body: Personalized recommendations, straight into your library.
howItWorks.step3Body: Log progress, join a club, talk it over with friends.

pricing.heading: Free means free
pricing.note: No credit card to sign up — ever.

founder.heading: Built by readers, for readers
founder.body: The Oracle started as one person's attempt to stop losing track of what to read next. It grew into a full reading companion — built the way we always wished a reading app already worked.

faq.heading: Before you dive in
faq.a1: Yes — creating an account, building your library, and getting recommendations from the Oracle costs nothing. No credit card required.
faq.q2: Can I import my existing Goodreads library?
faq.a2: Yes. Bring your Goodreads export and the Oracle sorts it straight into your wishlist and library.
faq.a3: Your library is private by default. You choose what to share with friends — nothing is public unless you make it so.
faq.q4: Can I use it in Spanish?
faq.a4: Sí — the Oracle speaks full Spanish, and you can switch languages anytime from the footer.
faq.q5: How does the Oracle make recommendations?
faq.a5: It reads your genres, moods, and the books you've rated, then match-scores your library instead of pushing whatever's trending.
faq.q6: Can I read with friends who don't have an account yet?
faq.a6: Yes — invite them to a book club or share a list with a link; they can view it before they sign up.

finalCta.heading: Your next book is waiting.
finalCta.sub: Join free and let the Oracle get to know your shelf.
